picks.rationale: MRCY shows explosive momentum with +49.82% YTD, +22.75% in the last month, a beta of 0.95, and positive news sentiment of 0.138. It is riding strong backlog expectations, and the recent price consolidation after a multi-period gain offers an attractive entry point.

RR.L delivers robust momentum with +59% 1-year return, +17.65% YTD, and +12.76% in the last month, backed by a solid P/E of 20.41 and a favourable analyst buy score of 1.60. The UK defence surge and nuclear propulsion catalyst support continued outperformance.

RTX combines a massive $271bn backlog, a recent $515m SPY-6 radar contract win with plans to double output by 2028, and a +6.81% 1-month move, while trading 14% below its 52-week high with a buy analyst score of 1.87 and positive news sentiment of 0.136.

TransDigm shows renewed momentum with a +12.66% 1-month gain and +5.62% in the last week, supported by a buy analyst score of 1.82 and positive news sentiment of 0.244, despite its high P/E of 41.5 which reflects its pricing power and unique aftermarket positioning.

Leonardo DRS has strong YTD performance of +33.02% with a low beta of 0.12, a compelling P/E of 43.07, and the highest news sentiment in the weapons segment at 0.343, reflecting favourable backlog and multi-year defence spending tailwinds.

Despite China export restrictions and recent volatility, RCAT has surged +24.89% YTD and +33.80% in the last month, with positive news sentiment of 0.176, driven by new Hellcat drone launch and Japan tactical ISR deployment program.

SAIC offers value with a P/E of 11.52 and a +1.89% YTD return, coupled with positive news sentiment of 0.135. The recent +7.42% monthly gain and short interest of 7.7% suggest potential squeeze dynamics amidst solid Q1 results.

HEICO delivers strong near-term momentum with a +15.30% one-month gain, a low beta of 1.035, and a buy analyst score of 1.83, underpinned by consistent demand in aerospace aftermarket and defence components.

AXON shows a reversal with a +7.92% one-month gain despite a -24.86% YTD drawdown, backed by a strong buy score (1.60) and positive news sentiment of 0.087. The high P/E of 169.36 is justified by its dominant position in law enforcement tech and recurring software revenue.

SPCX gains from high IPO demand and index inclusion catalysts (Russell 1000 & MSCI), with +14.94% YTD and +14.94% one-month return, though lockup expirations and volatility warrant caution. The recent pullback from highs may offer a re-entry for patient investors.

previous_picks.rationale: RR.L shows exceptional momentum with +57% 1-year return, +17.77% YTD, +21.04% 1-month, and a strong 'buy' analyst consensus (score 1.6). A P/E of 20.43 is reasonable given the growth, and the UK defence budget cycle provides a tailwind.

MRCY has surged +124% over 1 year, +49.82% YTD, and +22.75% in the last month, with a backlog growth catalyst driving the momentum. The P/E is negative (earnings not yet turned positive), but the strong multi-period gains and positive news sentiment indicate continued confidence.

RCAT has strong momentum (+67% 1-year, +24.89% YTD, +33.80% 1-month), and is a pure-play drone stock with high short interest (20.56%) that could squeeze. The P/E is negative as it is pre-profit, but the drone market tailwind is strong.

After a landmark IPO, SPCX has strong momentum (+15% 1-year, +14.94% YTD, +14.94% 1-month) with a favourable analyst consensus of 'buy' (score 1.83). Recent news highlights government contracts and a 46% rise from the offering price, though valuation remains extreme.
HEI has solid momentum (+7% 1-year, +2.40% YTD, +15.30% 1-month) and a buy consensus (score 1.83). A P/E of 60.2 is high but justified by consistent defence electronics growth and a strong backlog.

TDG shows a strong short-term rebound (+12.66% 1-month, +5.62% 1-week) and has a buy consensus (score 1.82). The P/E of 41.5 is acceptable for a market leader in aftermarket components, and the dividend yield supports the case.

RTX has a buy consensus (score 1.87) and a P/E of 34.9, below industry average, with positive news sentiment (0.224). Recent $US100m facility expansion for LTAMDS and Patriot systems, plus a $515m Navy contract, provide near-term catalysts, despite a slight YTD dip.

Despite a -46% 1-year return, AXON has bounced +7.92% in the last month and retains a buy consensus (score 1.6). A high P/E of 170.7 reflects growth expectations in AI-driven law enforcement tech, but recent momentum and a low earnings base justify the 'buy'.

SAIC has a P/E of 11.52 (low relative to peers) and positive YTD return (+1.89%), with a hold consensus (score 2.82). The lower valuation and a dividend yield of 2.33% (implied from £136) offer a defensive angle, though short interest is high (7.7%).

DRS has strong YTD performance (+33.02%) and positive news sentiment (0.343), with a P/E of 43.07 reflecting growth expectations in defence electronics. The recent momentum and backlog strength support the case, though analyst coverage is lacking.
